name: Dyson Supersonic Hair Dryer HD15 (Vinca Blue/Rosé)

specs.Motor: V9 digital motor in handle — 110,000 RPM
specs.Temperature: 13 precise air temperature settings — measures 40× per second
specs.Attachments: Flyaway smoother, smoothing nozzle, diffuser, styling concentrator, gentle air attachment
specs.Weight: 690g — motor in handle shifts balance point

whatWorks: Dyson's V9 motor spins at 110,000 RPM — nine times faster than typical hair dryer motors — and fits inside the handle rather than the barrel. The handle placement shifts the center of gravity, making the dryer balance differently in the hand with less wrist strain during long drying sessions. For daily users with long hair, this ergonomic difference is meaningful.

The glass bead thermistor measures air temperature 40 times per second and adjusts to prevent heat spikes. Conventional dryers output a fixed wattage regardless of proximity to hair, creating temperature variation that causes cumulative damage over time. The Supersonic's precision is genuinely protective for color-treated and fine hair.

13 temperature settings give you granular control over heat exposure. Most hair dryers offer 3 settings where the difference between 'hot' and 'medium' is 50°C. The Supersonic's 13 steps let you dial in exactly what your hair type needs, including a dedicated 'cold' temperature for setting styles without heat.

The included flyaway smoother attachment is the HD15's standout addition — it uses curved air channels to smooth surface flyaways without direct contact, replacing what typically requires a round brush while giving results that look more polished than any consumer dryer under $400.
worthKnowing: $699 CAD for a hair dryer is objectively a luxury purchase. The Shark HyperAir at $229 CAD and the Conair InfinitiPRO at $49 CAD both dry hair effectively. The Supersonic's premium is for precision heat technology, motor balance, specific attachment ecosystem, and long-term reliability — not raw drying speed.

The Supersonic is quieter than most salon dryers but not silent. Marketing language about being quieter can set unrealistic expectations — it sounds like a normal powerful dryer, just without the high-pitched motor whine. In absolute terms it's still a hair dryer.

The magnetic attachments are the most convenient system in the category — they click on and off instantly and never wobble. But they are Dyson-proprietary and won't work with other brands. If you already own Airwrap attachments, some are cross-compatible but not all.

Dyson's support and warranty in Canada is generally well-regarded, but repair costs outside warranty are high due to the specialized motor. Most users find it's worth buying a 2-year protection plan if one is available.

commonComplaints.title: Not worth $700 vs a $50 dryer…
commonComplaints.body: My hair dries fast. It looks good. But I genuinely cannot tell the difference from my old $60 Conair in terms of daily results. This is a luxury product and the premium is about feel, not function.

commonComplaints.title: The heat-sensing slows drying on thick hair…
commonComplaints.body: The temperature control is great but for my very thick hair it throttles back before my hair is fully dry. I end up using it longer than I expected.

commonComplaints.title: Replacement attachments are expensive…
commonComplaints.body: My concentrator nozzle cracked and a replacement from Dyson was $45. At this price point I expected the accessories to be more durable.

commonComplaints.title: Styling concentrator doesn't stay put…
commonComplaints.body: The magnetic attachment is convenient but the styling concentrator pops off if I angle the dryer downward too sharply. The other attachments stay on fine.

verdict: The Dyson Supersonic HD15 is the best hair dryer for anyone serious about long-term hair health — its precision heat control demonstrably reduces temperature damage compared to conventional dryers, and the handle-balanced motor makes extended styling sessions more comfortable. For daily users with fine, color-treated, or heat-damaged hair, it's a worthwhile investment. For occasional users or anyone without a specific concern about heat precision, the Conair InfinitiPRO at $49 CAD delivers 80% of the drying performance at 7% of the price.

realBuyers.quote: My hair feels so much healthier after switching. Six months in and I can see real improvement in texture and shine. The lower heat damage is not a marketing claim.
The flyaway smoother attachment is worth the upgrade alone. My blowout looks finished in a way it never did with my old dryer and round brush.
The handle balance is the thing nobody talks about. My wrist used to ache after drying my long hair. No problem with this.
Third Dyson dryer in 12 years. Each one lasted about 4 years of daily use. At the price it needs to — and each one has.

sources.title: Dyson Supersonic HD15 Hair Dryer — Amazon.ca

metaDescription: Dyson Supersonic HD15 hair dryer review for Canada: 110,000 RPM V9 motor, 13 temp settings, 5 attachments. ~$699 CAD. Honest on whether it's worth the price.
introText: The Dyson Supersonic HD15 hair dryer features a V9 digital motor spinning at 110,000 RPM housed in the handle for better balance, plus a thermistor that measures air temperature 40 times per second to protect against heat damage. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B0CLQMJ2YH) at around $699 CAD.
